era_context: Late Bronze Age, mid-18th Dynasty Egypt. Thutmose III is at the height of his military power. The Hittite Old Kingdom is consolidating territorial control. Mycenaean Greece is developing palace-centered societies. The Shang Dynasty in China is expanding its territorial control and refining bronze-working technology.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Central Asia (limited Hittite and Egyptian sources)

events.title: Continuation of Thutmose III's military campaigns
events.description: Thutmose III continues his systematic conquest and consolidation of Levantine territories. Egyptian military expeditions maintain control over previously conquered city-states and suppress rebellions. The pharaoh's military genius is reflected in his use of rapid deployment and strategic positioning of forces.
events.certainty_note: Thutmose III's campaigns are well-documented, but precise annual dating is uncertain.

events.title: Shang Dynasty bronze production advances
events.description: The Shang Dynasty continues to develop sophisticated bronze-casting techniques, producing ritual vessels (ding, gui) and weapons. Archaeological evidence from sites like Anyang shows increasing standardization and complexity in bronze metallurgy. These advances support both ritual and military functions.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Anyang and other Shang sites

events.certainty_note: Shang chronology is debated; this year is assigned within the documented Shang period, but precise dating is uncertain by ±20–50 years.

disconfirming_evidence: Shang Dynasty chronology remains one of the most contested issues in East Asian archaeology. Multiple competing chronological systems exist, with some scholars placing the Shang Dynasty 100+ years earlier or later than traditional dating. The dating of this year is therefore highly provisional.
historiographic_note: For this year, documentation is almost entirely dependent on Egyptian and Hittite elite sources, with archaeological evidence from the Levant and China providing supplementary information. The record is heavily skewed toward political and military events; economic, social, and cultural developments are underrepresented. Large regions of the world are entirely undocumented.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same campaign sequence as the previous year
